description: This figure presents the true stress–true strain curve of Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic steel, illustrating its strain hardening behavior under monotonic tensile loading at a strain rate of 1 mm/min. The curve demonstrates continuous work hardening from the origin up to approximately 1400 MPa at a true strain of 0.4. Key data points visible on the curve indicate true stress values of roughly 500 MPa at 0.05 strain, 800 MPa at 0.1 strain, 1000 MPa at 0.2 strain, and 1200 MPa at 0.25 strain, showing a transition from rapid initial hardening to a nearly linear hardening regime at higher strains. As stated in the caption and supported by the citation text, this exceptional strain hardening capacity is attributed to the austenitic structure's deformation mechanism involving mechanical twinning, where deformation twins act as barriers to dislocation motion and promote localized dislocation tangles. The curve provides fundamental mechanical property data for understanding the alloy's high endurance under elasto-plastic cyclic loading conditions described in the study of Fe-28Mn-9Al-0.86C steel.
